Key conclusions:
- The `production_ready` claim is honestly bounded (false / candidate-only / descriptor-verified) — no enterprise-trust overreach.
- Counts (150 units, distributions, phase/micro rollups) are internally consistent across plan, manifest, contract, registry, and baseline.
- No-write / no-real-data / descriptor-only boundaries are enforced in code (grep-confirmed) and mirrored in the attestation.
- Claude remains read-only and non-final; Hermes is used only for deterministic evidence-packet construction.
- Review hardening passed (auth preflight OK, no forbidden tools, summary-only correctly prohibited).

Two non-blocking P3 hygiene items for the implementer: a stale `source_email_dms_core_pack_id` field name (correct value, misleading label) and a self-contradicting path classification in `changed-file-scope.json` (authoritative manifest/active-refs are correct). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) supports the pack-level claim. The pack does NOT over-claim production readiness: production_ready is false across manifest.json and command-evidence.json (status implemented_pending_review); production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification token and Hermes production_ready_candidate is gated on coverage+foundation validity. Unit/deliverable/phase/micro counts (impl 104, contract 3, security_audit 6, ui 19, fixture 3, test 9, hermes_evidence 3, claude_review 3 = 150; P00=52, P01=98) are internally consistent across plan, manifest, contract, registry, and baseline. Grep confirmed the no-write boundary: zero writeFile/child_process/network/DB/object-storage calls in packages/time-expense; the no-write attestation (all runtime/write flags false, claude not promoted, no enterprise trust from local validation) is mirrored consistently in registry.js, service.js, validators.js and the contract. Claude is preserved as read-only, non-final reviewer (review-request read_only + Read/Grep/Glob, claude_not_final_approver true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval false). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet construction (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false), not product runtime. Review hardening is clean: auth_preflight passed (exit 0, non-empty valid JSON), prompt within size, forbidden tools absent, summary-only correctly prohibited (Risk C + security/review deliverables), and the forbidden_review_evidence list correctly encodes the CP178+ rejections (auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields). Tests are wired into npm test and the rp11:time-expense-core:validate gate ran green (exit 0). Two low-severity (P3) hygiene findings noted; neither affects correctness, boundaries, or the production_ready claim, so neither blocks pack or goal closeout. The value (CP00-341) is correct and upstream_program_id is correctly RP10, so this is a misleading template carryover, not a provenance/correctness error. The same misnamed field appears in packages/time-expense/src/registry.js (~line 91), src/service.js (~line 20), src/validators.js (~line 186), the contract (lines 34, 2186, 2306), and is asserted in scripts/validate-rp11-time-expense-core-contract.mjs (~line 103). The authoritative sources are correct and unaffected: active_pack_implementation_refs (lines 5-16) unambiguously lists the pack files, and the manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:6324-6332) excludes pack-owned paths.
